name: Balbum
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as handwritten, brush, and marker in style; the letterforms are casual and informal script, consistent with a brush or marker script subcategory.
summary: A casual brush and marker script by Alessia Mazzarella, released in a single variable style named Rolypoly.
description: Designed by Alessia Mazzarella and released in October 2020, Balbum is a handwritten script with a brush and marker character, available in a single variable style called Rolypoly. The family covers the Latin script and is supplied in OTF, TTF, WOFF and WOFF2 formats. Its informal, rounded quality suits packaging, editorial and display use where a hand-drawn warmth is wanted.
foundry_blurb: Typeland describe Balbum as a handwritten, brush and marker typeface designed by Alessia Mazzarella and released in October 2020. It covers the Latin script and is available in a single style, Rolypoly, with variable axes and OpenType features in the full commercial version.

name: Amifer
classification_notes: Typeland classify it as Sans with a quirky, informal character; its letterforms show humanist tendencies with idiosyncratic details that give it a distinctive personality rather than a neutral geometric or grotesque structure.
summary: A quirky, informal humanist sans by Alessia Mazzarella, released in 2020 across five weights with matching italics.
description: Designed by Alessia Mazzarella and released in October 2020, Amifer spans ten styles from Regular to Black with full italic companions. Its character sits between humanist warmth and informal idiosyncrasy, making it a reasonable choice for editorial, branding or interface work that calls for a sans with personality rather than neutrality. Variable font axes are available alongside static OTF, TTF, WOFF and WOFF2 formats.
foundry_blurb: Typeland describe Amifer as a sans typeface with a quirky, informal style, designed by Alessia Mazzarella and released in October 2020. It is available in ten styles covering the Latin script, with support for variable axes and OpenType features in the full versions.

name: Djaggety
classification_notes: Djaggety is explicitly described as pixelated, bitmap and jagged in style, with three distinct stylistic variants; these characteristics place it firmly in the display category with a pixel/bitmap subcategory.
summary: A pixelated, bitmap display family by Alessia Mazzarella comprising three distinct stylistic variants with variable axes.
description: Released in July 2025, the family splits across three styles — Pixel, Confetti and Blotter — each sharing a jagged, bitmap-derived aesthetic while offering its own character. All three styles include variable axes and OpenType features, and the family is supplied in OTF, TTF, WOFF and WOFF2 formats. Best suited to display-scale use in digital-native contexts where a raw, grid-locked quality is the point.
foundry_blurb: Typeland describe Djaggety as a pixelated, bitmap, jagged typeface by Alessia Mazzarella, released July 2025. The family comprises three styles — Pixel, Confetti and Blotter — each with variable axes and OpenType features, covering the Latin script and available in OTF, TTF, WOFF and WOFF2 formats.

name: Langar
classification_notes: Langar is described by the foundry as an upright-italic handwritten typeface, covering both Gurmukhi and Latin scripts, placing it firmly in the script/handwritten category with an unusual upright-italic character.
summary: A handwritten upright-italic typeface by Alessia Mazzarella, designed to work across both Gurmukhi and Latin scripts in a single style.
description: Released in November 2019, Langar is a single-weight typeface that pairs Gurmukhi and Latin in a unified handwritten voice. Its upright-italic character gives it an informal, calligraphic rhythm without leaning into conventional slanted script conventions. Available in OTF, TTF, WOFF and WOFF2, it is a relatively rare example of a hand-drawn face extending genuine coverage to the Gurmukhi script.
foundry_blurb: Typeland describe Langar as an upright-italic, handwritten typeface designed by Alessia Mazzarella and released in November 2019, supporting both Gurmukhi and Latin scripts. It is available in a single Regular style across OTF, TTF, WOFF and WOFF2 formats.

name: Frequencies
classification_notes: Classified as display due to its decorative uppercase-focused design with randomised letterforms derived from handdrawn children's canvases; not suited to body text and intended purely for expressive typographic use.
summary: A single decorative Latin face by Vaibhav Singh, drawn from children's handwriting collected for artist Oscar Murillo's 'Frequencies' archive project.
description: Originally designed for Oscar Murillo's 'Frequencies' archive, which gathered handdrawn canvases from school children around the world, the typeface translates that source material into a set of randomised decorative capitals. The style blurs the line between writing and drawing, with letterforms that carry a deliberate serendipity rather than a consistent underlying system. Available as a single style named Frittomisto, it is best suited to expressive display settings where that unpredictability is the point.
foundry_blurb: Typeland describe Frequencies as a typeface originally designed for Oscar Murillo's 'Frequencies' project archive, inspired by the handdrawn canvases of school children from around the world. The letterforms present serendipity and entangled frames of reference — imaginative translations without a fixed source — treating writing and drawing as an overlapping practice that accommodates divergences and interflows.

name: Zubtrak Stencil
classification_notes: Classified as a sharp stencil serif by the foundry; the stencil cuts interrupt otherwise serif letterforms, placing it firmly in the display stencil tradition with a serif skeleton.
summary: A sharp stencil serif in two weights by Vaibhav Singh, built for display use with a variable axis and a dry, angular character.
description: Released in October 2020, Zubtrak Stencil pairs a serif structure with hard stencil interruptions to produce a typeface suited to posters, editorial headlines and any context that wants a no-nonsense edge. It ships in Regular and Black, both with variable axes, and covers standard Latin in OTF, TTF, WOFF and WOFF2 formats. The foundry's own copy takes a pointed tone against type marketing clichés, which suits a face that is itself fairly blunt in its design language.
foundry_blurb: Typeland describe Zubtrak Stencil as a sharp stencil serif designed by Vaibhav Singh and released in October 2020. It is available in Regular and Black styles with variable axes, covering the Latin script in OTF, TTF, WOFF and WOFF2 formats.
